items.title: Family of seven's home left uninhabitable as insurer worsens damage

children.content: A Queensland family are living in an overcrowded caravan and shed, after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage in their home, initially caused by a leak.

items.title: 'Police every day': How school is different for Jewish Australian kids

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

image.altText: Rows of empty black desks, each with a white booklet on top and either brown or cream seats in a green room.

items.title: 'Not finished': Trump signals US strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain site

children.content: President Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex known as Pickaxe Mountain.

items.title: Labor members revive push for national gambling watchdog

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

items.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

image.altText: Pauline Hanson sits on stage talking during a question and answer session at a breakfact function, against a blue background.

items.title: Seized scam centre in Myanmar offers rare glimpse into industry targeting Australians

children.content: The scam industry in Myanmar is morphing and heading into the jungle rather than disappearing since a crackdown began.

items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

children.content: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby child protection review finds departmental failings

children.content: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, an interim report into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

items.title: The 'precious' bond driving the Stingers to success

children.content: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say as they call for licensing deals

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

items.title: 'Irresponsible': Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast

children.content: A UK minister has confirmed that a Russian naval vessel conducted a "live-fire weapons exercise" in international waters off the coast of Plymouth in south-west England.

items.title: Mother of identical quadruplets calls them her 'miracle babies'

children.content: A maternal foetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.

items.title: Protests turn violent after man dies during Italian police arrest

image.altText: Protesters holding up pictures of Abderrahim Fakir on an Italian street.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a light grey flat cap and black coat leavs court.

items.title: Armed with axes and a machete, ICE protester 'attacked' US federal building, FBI says

children.content: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

items.title: India steps up response to US, Iran deadly ship strikes

children.content: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Officer handcuffed teen in lead-up to police station rape, court told

children.content: A NSW Police officer locked a 17-year-old in a cell and handcuffed her on separate occasions before he sexually assaulted her in a police station in 1998, a court has heard.

items.title: Inquest to determine whether man killed his mother and staged her death

children.content: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

items.title: Ian doesn't want children. He's part of a growing trend among men

children.content: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

image.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

items.title: Australian EV owners deal with 'range anxiety'. China has almost eliminated it

children.content: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

items.title: Philippines, China clash in South China Sea

children.content: Vision posted online by the Philippines Coast Guard shows Filipino and Chinese personnel briefly engaging each other from inflatable boats, with the Philippines accusing China of assaulting one of its navy staff.

image.altText: An image shows inflatable boats with personnel from the Philippine Navy clashing with personnel from China's Coast Guard.

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest
